title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. inconcinna
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. inconcinna, also known as the lady's mantle or alpine lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the plant Alchemilla vulgaris, which belongs to the Rosaceae family of flowering plants. It is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including soothing menstrual cramps, reducing anxiety and stress, and promoting digestive health. Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. inconcinna is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be found in skincare products and creams due to its antioxidant properties.
